title: Group-aware](https://www.kaggle.com/code/mohankrishnathalla/ps-s6e5-full-eda-feature-engineering-models%22]},{%22title%22:%22Group-aware) validation suite
summary: Replace single random holdout selection with a validation report spanning stratified random folds, Race-Year grouped folds, and season-holdout or race-holdout stress tests.
rationale: The run's local and submitted outcomes did not always agree qualitatively, and many feature variants were duplicate-heavy. A broader validation suite is needed to distinguish real generalization from random-split memorization of race and year contexts.
implementation_hint: For each candidate solution, compute OOF AUC under StratifiedKFold, StratifiedGroupKFold grouped by Race-Year, and a small set of leave-year or leave-race stress folds. Use random folds for continuity but select hypotheses by consistency across folds rather than by a single score.
expected_effect: Large improvement in experiment reliability; direct AUC may not increase immediately, but selected models should be more robust.
risk: Grouped validation can understate performance if the public test is randomly sampled from the same Race-Year contexts. Treat it as a robustness diagnostic, not the only selection criterion.
